briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. I will begin today's climate briefing on April 29, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Crisis, Becoming a Reality: Deepening Impacts and Warnings
sections.content: The direct impacts of climate change are becoming increasingly evident both domestically and internationally. Over 60% of Gyeongnam residents reported directly experiencing climate change and suffering from stress [3, 4], and the concentrations of the four major greenhouse gases on the Korean Peninsula once again reached record highs [8]. Europe is warming twice as fast as the global average, a trend confirmed by new Copernicus data [6, 51]. Furthermore, with reports of climate-altering gases being emitted from reservoirs [54], and dozens of homes being lost to coastal erosion in North Carolina [74], the severity of the climate crisis's impact on daily life and the environment is repeatedly being warned about [7].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic and International Policy and Scientific Response Efforts
sections.content: Amidst this crisis, policy and scientific efforts to address climate change continue both domestically and internationally. At South Korea's southernmost atmospheric monitoring station, climate change-causing substances are being monitored through wind observation [1], and Pohang City operated a Climate Change Week and conducted a climate action campaign in commemoration of Earth Day [9]. The Presidential Committee on National Climate Crisis Response is active [12], and domestically and internationally, the importance of education is also being emphasized, including the proposal of climate change education laws [50, 62]. Meanwhile, a climate change summit was held in Colombia, fostering active international discussions [63].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Industrial Progress
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy for achieving carbon neutrality is accelerating across all industries. Korea Midland Power (KOMIPO) is operating its Renewable Energy Operations Division and establishing an AI-based smart management system [25, 28, 30, 31, 34, 42], while Korea Western Power (KOWEPO) and Korea East-West Power (EWP) have also embarked on expanding their renewable energy organizations and developing flow batteries [24]. The Daegu Regional Environmental Office signed an agreement for renewable energy transition in the Gumi National Industrial Complex [29], and domestic companies are actively expanding overseas, with Hyundai Engineering successfully securing 460 billion won in project financing for a North American solar power development project [40, 44]. The news of solar power companies collectively turning a profit in the first quarter indicates a positive trend in the renewable energy market [36].

sections.title: 🌱 Carbon Neutrality Practices and Community Adaptation Strategies
sections.content: Local communities and businesses are making various efforts to adapt to climate change and practice carbon neutrality. Daejeon Urban Development Corporation has introduced landscape designs based on tree carbon calculators [2], and the head of the Korea Forest Service has fully launched climate change response policies at arboretum sites [5]. The introduction of the Korean Green Taxonomy has strengthened the transparency of corporate carbon neutrality activities [11], and technological innovation continues, with Samsung Electronics launching new carbon-neutral heat pump boiler products [13]. In four cities and counties within Chungcheongnam-do, including Gongju and Seosan, 'Climate Crisis Safe Villages' are being further developed to enhance community resilience to climate change [19, 20], and various local governments, such as Gwangmyeong and Goyang cities, are encouraging carbon neutrality practices through environmental education and cleanup activities for citizens [10, 22, 23].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing demonstrates that despite the severity of the climate crisis, domestic and international efforts for renewable energy transition and carbon neutrality are expanding. We hope for your continued interest and participation in responding to the climate crisis. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Hyundai Engineering Secures 460 Billion KRW for U.S. Solar Power Project
articles.original_title: 현대엔지니어링, 미 태양광 사업 4600억원 확보
articles.description: Hyundai Engineering announced on the 29th that it has signed a project financing (PF) agreement worth approximately $310 million (about 460 billion KRW) with four domestic and international financial institutions, including Korea Development Bank. With the secured funds, Hyundai Engineering plans to commence construction of the 'Hillsborough Solar Power Plant' project in the U.S. during the first half of this year. The lending group includes KDB and Crédit Agricole CIB.

summary.phenomenon: On the 29th, Hyundai Engineering signed a project financing (PF) agreement worth approximately $310 million (about 460 billion KRW) with four domestic and international financial institutions, including Korea Development Bank. These funds are earmarked for the 'Hillsborough Solar Power Plant' project in the U.S.
summary.outlook: Hyundai Engineering plans to commence construction on the Hillsborough Solar Power Plant project in the U.S. during the first half of this year, utilizing the secured funds.

articles.original_description: 산은 등 4개 금융기관과 PF 약정현대엔지니어링은 한국산업은행 등 국내외 4개 금융기관과 약 3억1000만달러(약 4600억원) 규모의 프로젝트 파이낸싱(PF) 금융약정을 체결했다고 29일 밝혔다. 현대엔지니어링은 이번 약정으로 확보한 재원으로 미국 ‘힐스보로 태양광발전소’ 사업을 올해 상반기 착공한다. 대주단으로 참여한 곳은 산업은행과 크레디아그리콜 CI···

articles.original_title: ‘Suicidal’ model of capitalism leading to war and fascism, climate summit told
articles.description: Colombia president Gustavo Petro tells 57-country talks on a green energy transition that fossil fuel interests could destroy humanityThe world is threatened by a “suicidal” model of capitalism that is leading to war, fascism and the potential extinction of humanity, Colombia’s president has said, as he convened 57 governments to address the climate crisis.Gustavo Petro blamed fossil fuel interests for taking ever more desperate measures to prevent a transition to green energy. “There is inertia

summary.phenomenon: Colombia's President Gustavo Petro stated at a climate summit involving 57 countries that the world is threatened by a 'suicidal' model of capitalism.
summary.cause: President Petro blamed fossil fuel interests for taking desperate measures to prevent a green energy transition, asserting that this is the cause of the 'suicidal' capitalist model.
summary.outlook: President Petro warned that this model could lead to war, fascism, and the potential extinction of humanity.

articles.title: Labor will back fossil fuels in the budget but the gas tax campaign isn’t dead yet | Clear air
articles.description: Despite popular support for a 25% levy on gas exports, Anthony Albanese opposes it and there is little discussion about how to reduce our usageSign up for climate and environment editor Adam Morton’s free Clear Air newsletter hereFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesWe’re yet to see next month’s Australian federal budget, so mark this column with an asterisk. But barring a late change it is likely it will do a remarkable thing: give fossil fuel industries what they want.That’s w

summary.phenomenon: In the upcoming Australian federal budget, the Labor government is expected to support the fossil fuel industry despite popular support for a 25% levy on gas exports.
summary.cause: This is because Prime Minister Anthony Albanese opposes the gas export levy, and there is little discussion about how to reduce gas usage.

articles.original_title: Dozens of North Carolina houses have been lost to the sea. Some surviving homes are now being moved on wheels
articles.description: Pace of sea-level rise has turned Outer Banks coastal area into a ‘canary in the coalmine’ for other east coast communitiesMoving house has a more literal meaning on Hatteras Island, the slender hook of land that juts off the coast of North Carolina. After a slew of houses toppled spectacularly into the Atlantic Ocean recently, entire buildings are now being lifted on to wheels to flee the rapidly eroding coastline.Since September, 19 homes have been lost to waves that tore them from their pilin

summary.phenomenon: Since September, on Hatteras Island, North Carolina, 19 homes have been lost to waves, detached from their foundations and carried into the Atlantic Ocean. Surviving homes are now being moved on wheels to escape the rapidly eroding coastline.
summary.cause: The pace of sea-level rise has turned the Outer Banks coastal area into a 'canary in the coalmine', as waves destroy homes and the coastline rapidly erodes.
summary.outlook: The Outer Banks region serves as a 'canary in the coalmine' for other East Coast communities, suggesting that similar impacts could occur elsewhere.
